review_text: Queenstown library is a heritage building, rich in its own history. Spacious with two levels, kids area is on the first floor. The second floor for adults is really quiet with nice view over a green park.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
building's exterior may look unassuming or even a lil spooky but the inside is really cozy and homely! lovely space, limited tables but plenty of seats

if you really want a table, they have a table booking system you can use to ensure you have one when you get there!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Nostalgia a plenty in this well preserved library that houses many childhood memories for some.

Located in the quiet side of Queenstown facing sky residences @ Dawson, short walk from Queenstown MRT, a cozy library to spend your time here.

2 levels served by a lift & stairs, lots of reading areas and I particularly enjoy the level 2 quiet reading room facing the greenery.

There are lots of nostalgia and nice touches to this library.

Take your time to explore, read, borrow or exchange. what a privilege!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As of April 2026: It is a old school library with the kinda of nostalgia you get when you visited a library in the 2000's.

Most of the Library's floor space is dedicated to books with working space being rather limited. It's not very convenient to access either as you gotta walk abit from the MRT station and not much amenities nearby.

The tables tend to get booked out until like 7pm ish. With patrons playing musical chairs where someone who has a booking asks those who didn't make a booking to move.

Interesting mix of patrons, with elderly men being the majority and somehow quite a few expats.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: The only bus Interchange that is sandwiched between Hajjah Rahimabi Kebun Limau Mosque, Whampoa Park, and Whampoa River, this bus Interchange serves the residents of Whampoa linking it to Aljunied (loop at Sims Avenue), Bukit Merah, HarbourFront, Pasir Ris, Shenton Way, and Tampines North. The nature of the bus Interchange is that it is divided into two bus islands to cater more spaces for large buses parking at this tiny Interchange. There is also a public food court and small public parking space in front.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Hidden away in the Rifle Range greenery, Temasek Club honestly feels like one of those underrated chill spots in Singapore. The whole place has a peaceful resort vibe with lots of nature surrounding it, making it great for family gatherings, weddings, casual dinners or even just escaping the city for awhile.

The atmosphere is probably the biggest selling point here. Many visitors mention the relaxing ambience, lush views and spacious environment that makes it feel less hectic than most clubs in Singapore. There is also a decent Cantonese restaurant within with very nice menu options.

One thing to note is accessibility. If you are driving, parking is manageable and convenient. But if you rely on public transport, getting there can feel slightly out of the way since the club sits deeper into Rifle Range Road.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: On 6 March 2026, I had the privilege of visiting Temasek Club during a Taiwan–Singapore exchange meeting. The venue is beautiful and well maintained, creating a welcoming and refined atmosphere. The service was professional and attentive, and the food was excellent. It was a very pleasant and memorable experience.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Here is one MRT station that seniors can pass through safely. There is even ONE parking lot for personal mobility device just next to the entrance. Very considerate.

Mostly deserted except during peak hours. No queues at ATMs, topping up stations - and clean restrooms.

Conveniently located next to the historical Haw Par Villa.

Huge crowds used to throng it for its Chinese mythological figurines and depiction of "hell" punishment chambers. But alas, not anymore - even after reverting back to free admission.

Guess times have changed. Singapore have changed. Values have changed as well.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: One of the tourist spots. Now not so popular but tourists should visit at least once.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
I recently attended a small group circuit training session at Trilogic Fitness in Novena and had a great experience.

The gym is spacious, fully air-conditioned, and conveniently located at the IRAS Building, just a short walk from Novena MRT station.

Eugene and his team were incredibly knowledgeable, supportive, and attentive throughout the session. They took the time to ensure everyone was performing the exercises with proper form and selected exercises that would help us build the conditioning needed for our upcoming challenge.

Their dedication, expertise, and encouragement made the session both effective and enjoyable. We are very happy with the outcome and would like to thank Eugene and the entire Trilogic Fitness team for their excellent support. Highly recommended!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
My first time here today and was awed by the size and spaciousness of the gym. It has the best of both worlds; strength and cardio areas. Also hyrox stations if that’s your thing! Also there’s an area for physiotherapy and workout classes. It’s quite about everything you can ask for in a gym, and work out without feeling self-conscious. place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Very nice place to either have a slow walk, trail walk, or trail run. There are challenging slopes & steps, but there are also friendly sections to catch your breath. Distance wise, one can choose to walk from a short 3 KM route or a longer 6KM+ route. With the terrain, it is a good place to burn some calories and at the same time, enjoy the greenery with some  sightings of animals in the wild. So far, I have encountered calugo, blue diamond coral snake, pangolin, wild boar, monitor lizards, drongo (bird) and of course, monkeys (plenty of them).

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Went hiking at Bukit Timah and Rifle Range and it turned out to be a really refreshing experience. The trail was surrounded by tall trees and thick greenery, making the whole walk feel calm and peaceful. As I went down the wooden steps, I could hear the sounds of nature all around me—birds, insects, and the rustling of leaves.
Along the way, I noticed small details that made the hike even more special, like tiny plants growing out of old wood and patches of moss spreading across fallen logs. It reminded me how alive the forest is, even in the smallest places.
The hike wasn’t too easy, especially with the long paths and steps, but it felt satisfying. I managed to cover about 7.8 kilometers, and even though I was tired, it was a good kind of tired. Overall, it was a simple but meaningful experience—just being surrounded by nature, slowing down, and enjoying the moment.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: The highest hill in Sg but there is no view at the summit. A little disappointing. Very hot and humid. Prefer the treetop trail at Macritchi reservoir.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Balestier | Student Care Balestier
review_text:
My kid enjoys being at PF Balestier and asks to be picked up later in the day, rather than earlier! She enjoys being taught by Ms Wei Ling and has made many good friends in the centre. The staff, including Ms Claudia, Ms Kausalya, Ms Jas, and the support staff, are caring and take good care of the children.
Homework and spelling are completed in the centre. Holiday programmes are also fun, with interesting excursions.
Staff are responsive to queries and messages, giving parents a peace of mind.
Will recommend.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I was here today (17 DECEMBER 2025) arriving from Newton MRT Station, after alighting thereat via the Downtown Line from Hillview Station. I live in the Hillview area and this MRT Station is a very convenient one for me to alight at, to go to my two adjacent to each other favourite shopping malls of Velocity @ Novena Square and Square 2 @ Novena. And while this MRT Station is quite an old one, it is well maintained and the ambience therein is comfortable in that it is well ventilated.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: We needed a 4 day visitor pass and they dont sell at this stop. Have to go to Orchard station, problem is the MRT no longer sells tickets and you have to pay to get to Orchard station. Finally a super friendly guy helped us. Note to visitors DO NOT buy visitor pass at Changi airport.  A four day pass is $37. At Changi they wanted  $52.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I stayed at the Oasia Hotel.  The hotel basement is connected to the station which makes commuting less of a hassle.

It’s a busy station that's connected to a mall with some  F&B eateries. Also it has  easy access  to places at Novena like square 2, Royal medical centre, novena medical centre, Mount Elizabeth Hospital.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
🟢 Novena MRT station (NS20) is an underground train station. 🔴It is part of the North South Line.

⬛️ from Novena Station, the train goes 2 ways.
- All the way to Jurong East ( West region)
- down south to Marina South Pier.

🟦 Novena Station is located at Thomson Road in Novena district. 🟡 It is the nearest MRT station to hospitals like Tan Tock Seng Hospital & Mount Elizabeth Novena Hospital.

🟪 Malls like Novena Square, Velocity mall, Royal Square and United Square malls are located in close proximity to Novena Station.

🟡 The Inland Revenue dept ( IRAS) is just sited opposite to this station.

🟢 Novena station was built on a former Jewish cemetery, and the station was completed in 1988. I know because I live around this area when I was a child.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Visited in the late morning before the weather got too hot , historical park with heritage buildings & a gallery of history of this place was event fully refreshing .

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
My go to whenever I fly through T4 to get a quick bite. Quick, no frills, okay staff, and it has minced ginger, something that many chicken rice stalls have forgo, which is a shame.

The food is decent, price is okay for airport foodcourt. A basic roasted chicken rice is $5.5 while additional like braised egg is $1. Juat like other stall, ,there are special prices for staff.

There are other sets to choose from.

Novena Square has went through many improvements over the decades. Now it connencted to other shopping malls like United Square, Velocity, and Square2.

It's quite nice to shop there during the weekend morning because this area is mostly populated by working crowd during working days.

There are many sport shops, because Velocity is intended to be some kind of sport mall. There is a wall climbing on the top floor of Novena Square. Besides that, F&B, pharmacies, Cold Storage, bakeries, banks, toy shops, courses can be found here.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Went to Tcm here and the standards of cleanliness is still v high. V impressive. Keep up the good work guys! Air con was fresh and crisp and u could feel the cooling sensation. Attached are how clean looking the place is and how clean the lifts are

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: My wife and I were here today (17 DECEMBER 2025) for lunch and for shopping. We often come to this shopping mall via the North South Line alighting at the Novena MRT Station that is beneath this shopping mall and which is conveniently connected thereto via escalators or a flight of staircase. My wife and I love coming to this shopping mall for its very good eating places and retail stores therein. We also find this shopping mall to be well maintained and to afford a pleasant dining and shopping ambience therein with its wide enough aisles and cool air-conditioning.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: buzzling mall. I like the shopscand food outlets here. very nicely furnished and inviting.

review_text: Good mall in Novena. Has most outlets for food and shopping. Decathlon, Uniqlo, Saladstop, Liv Activ, Mr Coconut. Connected to Novena MRT station and bus top.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Teacher Natalie is one of those teachers that students genuinely enjoy learning from. Ally always looks forward to her lessons and feels encouraged to do better after every class.

What we appreciate most is not just the academic guidance, but the positive and motivating classroom environment she creates. Even when the mock papers were challenging, the students stayed engaged and motivated because of her teaching style and encouragement.

We were especially happy to hear that Ally’s effort in reviewing her mistakes helped her achieve the highest score in class. Thank you, Teacher Natalie and The Learning Lab, for making learning both meaningful and enjoyable for the student

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: My son has been in TLL since primary 1 and since then, I never regretted signing up for TLL. TLL teaches topics in advance and teaches techniques to hone your answering skills, especially in Primary 5 and 6 where the workload and demand can get pretty overwhelming. In P6, all worksheets provided by TLL were helpful and benefited my son’s learning.Each worksheet had a section it focused so if my son wants to study a specific section, he can easily study it with the TLL worksheets. In PSLE, he achieved an AL6. TLL gave him so many techniques to use in exams and it was without a doubt the key reason why he achieved this score.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Very disappointing experience. The teaching quality was poor, and instead of providing proper support and a clear learning plan, the teachers kept complaining about the student’s level without offering real solutions or structured guidance.

We enrolled expecting a professional support centre, but most of the responsibility was pushed back onto the parents, with constant requests for support from home — as if the student was in a regular school, not a centre specialised in learning support.

Communication and follow-up were also very poor. Replies were slow, updates were limited, and there was little genuine care or attention towards the student’s progress.

Unfortunately, the overall service was far below expectations.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Mr Welson Lee taught my daughter science from Primary 4 to primary 6. He has high efficiency, is very patient and is very kind. Whenever science questions that my daughter does not know how to solve are sent to him online, he always replies in time. With Mr Lee's help, my daughter's science has improved a lot, getting AL1 for science in PSLE.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Would like to drop a note of appreciation to Teacher Deborah for guiding my boy to AL1 for English. Really appreciate Teacher Deborah's dedication, patience & the way she helped him grow more confident in the subject.
We are grateful that she always updates us, and clearly points out his strengths and areas to improve. Even online, she makes sure every child gets equal attention. Very thankful for her hard work.

Would like to drop a note of appreciation to Teacher Deborah for guiding my boy to AL1 for English. Really appreciate Teacher Deborah's dedication, patience & the way she helped him grow more confident in the subject.
We are grateful that she always updates us, and clearly points out his strengths and areas to improve. Even online, she makes sure every child gets equal attention. Very thankful for her hard work.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

review_text:
Several good eateries, bustling at the weekend. Convenient location across the road from the dragon playground. We enjoyed the family fun day.
Quite nice nursing room.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Very well maintained. It has Food (Mcdonald, Yakun, Restautant) , Sports (Swimming pool, Squash court, Multi Sports halll) , Tuition centre (Berries, Ballet, Physics), Korean Haircut shop...

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Bowling was fun. Helpful staff that helped us with setting up the lanes for us. Restaurant on-site had a wide range of finger foods to make our little celebration a cheerful event.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Very big and spacious safra with many facilities. 1 Olympic sized pool with 2 baby pools. On a weekday, the pool is relatively empty as there are not many jobless people swimming on a weekday afternoon. They have MacDonalds and Yakun as well. We went to play the darts which is located inside the KTV room on lvl 2. There is a billiard room beside it and a bowling room on top.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: I liked ❤️ to compliment Vini at the counter upon my visit on 16 June 2026 on weekdays at about 530pm as she served me with a great passion she helpfully check my almost soon to be expiry membership well and I will love to sign ✍️ up to renew in future as the space and surroundings was pleasant everything change here good idea for a meeting and events set up ⬆️ free wifi internet 🛜 worth visiting here!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The school has a warm and inviting atmosphere with an expansive outdoor area - big plus point ! The teachers and staff in Etonhouse Newton are  nurturing, kind and approachable. That definitely shows in how the children are settled, always cheerful & engaged! Thank you for the wonderful experience

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Our kid has been at this kindergarten for almost a year, and we’re so glad we chose it. At first there was a bit of separation anxiety, but now they’re always excited to go every morning. The teachers are super caring and patient, and the environment feels really warm. We can see our child becoming more confident, independent, and caring — it really feels like a second home for them.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We have had a truly wonderful experience at EtonHouse Newton. The school provides a nurturing and encouraging environment where children feel seen, heard, and loved.

We are deeply grateful to the dedicated teachers and community for the care, guidance, and warmth they show every day. Thank you for shaping happy little humans 💛

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My experience with EtonHouse Newton has been nothing short of positive. The space felt calm, welcoming, and thoughtfully put together.

Nothing seemed without intention—which reflects the school’s commitment to the IB curriculum, not just in theory but in how it shapes daily interactions and learning experiences.

The classrooms were among my favourite parts of the school. Each had its own style, highlighting the individuality of both teachers and children—something I wish I had experienced more of growing up.

Altogether, it left me with the impression of a place that truly values not just how children learn, but how they feel while learning. I’d highly recommend dropping by, you’ll meet some of the kindest people I’ve ever come across.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As a family PA, I frequently communicated with EtonHouse Newton because my boss’s child attends the school.  I’ve witnessed the remarkable growth of my boss’s daughter into a sweet, loving, and respectful young woman under EtonHouse’s care.

The teachers at EtonHouse Newton are truly exceptional. They are caring, attentive, and nurturing, and their operations, parent liaison, and education are exceptionally impressive.  Every staff member at EtonHouse Newton, including the helpful admin personnel like Ms. Keisha, is prompt and professional in responding to my inquiries about school matters.

Thank you, EtonHouse, for shaping our little princess and her elder brother into the wonderful individuals they are today. Your hard work in preparing their foundation has undoubtedly played a crucial role in their future success.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

review_text: One-North is a very well-structured MRT station, perfectly servicing one of Singapore's major business and industrial districts. The layout is refreshingly straightforward, making it a station where you're unlikely to ever be late due to navigation issues. With multiple exits that integrate directly into the surrounding buildings, it’s a 'get the job done' kind of stop that feels custom-built for the busy professionals working in the area.

review_text:

🟨 9 Ayer Rajah Ave, Singapore 138647

🟦One-North Station is connected to the integrated office/retail complexes of Fusionopolis, Connexis, Symbiosis and Biopolis and other office towers.

🟧 The Insead Business School is in close proximity to the One-North station.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: This MRT station largely mirrors the standard efficiency and cleanliness expected of Singapore's rail network. My only significant reservation lies with the current state of the surrounding area, which is unfortunately quite messy due to ongoing, seemingly endless road works.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Paragon has been a familiar hangout spot for me for many years, and it’s still one of Orchard Road’s most dependable malls. It has a classy, spacious feel and remains a great place to spend time whether you’re shopping, meeting friends, or just taking a slow walk indoors

There’s a strong mix of retail stores, good eateries, and practical conveniences all under one roof. I also like that it has Cold Storage Gold / Fresh for quality groceries, which makes it more than just a shopping mall. The presence of medical clinics adds to the convenience too

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is a modern upscale 6 level shopping mall right in the heart of Orchard Road. Most people who shop here knows what they want and they just go straight to the shop. Others come here for their lunch or simply have tea here for nice chit chats. Now’s the time for Chinese New Year shopping so the mall is beautifully decorated this year !! I love the beautiful lights !!! And can feel the Chinese New Year vibes and mood !!! I came here to buy my CNY clothes at Metro Paragon on level two. It’s been a wonderful and fruitful trip !! Bought 6 pieces 👍👍👍💗💗💗

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A wonderful and good shopping experience having good brands like Prada and Gucci in them. It also has amazing food and restaurant options on the basement level. The best food you could find is the fish and Coast company which serves authentic fish to perfection and also the area has some good Italian food joints, and some nice Asian and local cuisine as well. It also has a nice supermarket to buy daily essentials and fresh fruits.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Stepping into Paragon feels like entering a high-end gallery. The layout is spacious and intuitive, featuring polished marble floors and a glass-fronted facade that has become a landmark on Orchard Road.

​Key Highlights
-​Luxury Fashion: The mall is synonymous with high fashion, housing iconic multi-story flagships for Gucci, Prada, and Givenchy, alongside luxury heavyweights like Saint Laurent, Balenciaga, and Loewe.

-​The "Junior" Floor: Level 5 is a dedicated sanctuary for families, featuring high-end children’s labels like Armani Junior and Petit Bateau, complemented by a free indoor playground and top-tier enrichment centers.

-​Exquisite Dining: From the Michelin-starred Crystal Jade Golden Palace to the legendary Imperial Treasure Super Peking Duck, the culinary offerings lean heavily toward quality. For something more casual yet chic, PS.Cafe and the basement's Din Tai Fung are perennial favorites.

-​Medical Wellness: Uniquely, the mall is connected to Paragon Medical, a premium tower housing world-class specialists and aesthetic clinics, allowing visitors to combine wellness appointments with luxury retail.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Paragon Shopping Centre is one of my favourite malls along Orchard Road when I feel like doing some upscale shopping. I enjoy browsing the luxury brands here—it’s elegant but still comfortable to walk around. The basement has plenty of nice food choices too, whether it’s grabbing a quick bite or sitting down for a proper meal. I also like that the mall has a supermarket and even a medical wing with specialist clinics, so it’s really convenient for almost everything.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Mustafa Centre is an incredible shopping destination! I always find exactly what I need there, and the variety of items available is truly amazing. 😊 The atmosphere is lively, and it's a great place to explore. So many unique finds! 🛍️ Love it! ✨

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
A 24/7 Shopping Paradise with Great Prices! 🛒🇸🇬
Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Mustafa Centre is one of the most famous shopping malls in Singapore, and for a good reason. It’s open 24/7, making it incredibly convenient for anyone to shop at any time of the day or night.
Why I recommend it:
• Best Prices: Almost all goods are sold at very reasonable and competitive prices.
• One-Stop Shop: From electronics and jewelry to groceries and souvenirs, you can find everything here. It’s the go-to spot for foreigners and migrant workers to buy gifts and essentials for their families back home before vacation.
Important Tip for Shoppers:
I highly recommend checking the expiry dates on all food and consumable items before you buy. Try to ensure they have at least one year of validity.
My Experience: I’ve had issues multiple times where I bought items in bulk, but since the expiry date was too close, I couldn't finish them in time. This is a big waste of money, so please double-check your basket before heading to the cashier!
#MustafaCentre #SingaporeShopping
#LittleIndia
#TravelTips
#BudgetShopping #SmartShopping

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
I have been to Singapore several times, but it was my first time going there.
I really regret that I didn't go there before.
There are many snacks, accessories, cosmetics and so on.
As a tourist, I could have reasonable souvenirs.
I will go there next time when I visit Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Mustafa Centre is one of the most unique shopping experiences in Singapore. Located in the heart of Little India, this massive multi-level store literally has everything — from electronics and perfumes to groceries, souvenirs, jewelry, clothing, and even travel essentials.
What makes it even more impressive is that it’s open 24 hours a day. Whether you need something late at night or just enjoy browsing without crowds during off-peak hours, it’s incredibly convenient.
The store can feel a bit overwhelming at first because it’s packed floor-to-ceiling with products and narrow aisles, but that’s part of its charm. Prices are competitive, and you can often find great deals, especially on chocolates, cosmetics, and electronics.
If you’re staying in the Little India area or nearby, it’s definitely worth a visit — even if just to see how massive and diverse the selection is. A true Singapore experience for shoppers.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Mammoth! You name it and you'll have it. It spans across the street and variety. Reasonable prices and lot of variety. They have every retail brand under the sky here. Electronics, apparels, grocery, fresh produce, jewellery what not. Hats off to the management. If you are looking for best prices in Singapore this is the place to go.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
This club offers a wide range of facilities, making it the perfect spot for both relaxation and active recreation.

🏊 Swimming Pool – The pool is well-maintained, clean, and spacious. Perfect for both casual swimmers and those looking to get in some serious laps.
🎳 Bowling Alley – A fun and well-kept facility with great lanes, making it ideal for both beginners and experienced bowlers.
🎾 Tennis Courts – The courts are in excellent condition, and booking was smooth. Great place to enjoy a game with friends.
🧗 Wall Climbing – A highlight of the club! The climbing walls cater to different skill levels, and the instructors are friendly and helpful.
🎉 Function Rooms – Well-equipped and spacious, making them perfect for private gatherings, meetings, and celebrations.

The overall atmosphere of the club is fantastic, with a welcoming staff and well-maintained amenities. Whether you're here for sports, relaxation, or social events, CSC has everything you need. Highly recommended!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: ION Orchard delivers a high-end shopping experience right in the heart of Orchard Road. The architecture is striking, and the mall houses a wide range of luxury brands alongside stylish dining spots. It’s always lively and well-maintained, though it can feel overwhelming due to its size. Still, one of the most iconic retail destinations in Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: It wasn't a goal on my list. I visited it only because through it you have access to ION Sky, an observation deck. What I liked is the absolutely unique architecture, very beautiful. Otherwise, it has nothing extra compared to other malls.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A huge, modern, expensive shrine to consumerism. If you have the money, and yoy need lots,  you can find anything here. We did however have the best lunch date at Twinings much to my surprise and delight. I would absolutely go there again.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: ION Orchard is a premier shopping destination in Singapore, known for its futuristic design and luxury brands. Located on Orchard Road, it offers a mix of high-end boutiques, popular retail stores, and great dining options. The ION Sky observation deck provides beautiful city views, making it more than just a shopping mall—it’s an experience.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Full of high end stores. Can rest a little while inside when you’re a bit tired. The structure was amazing.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
It had been years since I last stepped foot in this library, as I hardly visit Toa Payoh.
For those who enjoy a touch of history and nostalgia, don’t miss the old photos along the staircase. They showcase the history of Toa Payoh town, Toa Payoh Town Park, and the library itself. It’s really interesting, and I think all libraries should have something like this so residents can learn more about the history of their own town. Thumbs up 👍

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I’d describe Toa Payoh Library as one of those “just nice” neighborhood libraries — not massive and overwhelming, but definitely not cramped either. It’s an easy few-minute walk from the MRT, which already makes it super convenient, and the location is a big plus since Toa Payoh Mall is right nearby. If you ever feel like grabbing a snack, bubble tea, or a proper meal before or after studying, you’re honestly spoiled for choice.

One detail I really liked was the stairwell — instead of being plain and boring, it’s illustrated with little bits of information about Toa Payoh and libraries, which adds a nice local touch and makes the walk between floors more interesting. The layout is also very straightforward: the first floor is dedicated to children, the second floor is for adults, and the third floor is for teens and the programming or activity zones.

Both the adult and teen sections have a good amount of seating, and what’s even better is that the seats are usually available. I didn’t need to make any reservations, and it didn’t feel stressful trying to hunt for a spot. It’s a comfortable, practical place to read, study, or just spend a quiet afternoon without feeling rushed or crowded.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Toa Payoh Library is more than just a place for books; it’s a time capsule. Growing up here, the library and the iconic Dragon Playground nearby were the backdrops of my childhood. I am so glad that as the library has modernized, the effort to preserve its essence—and the heritage of the surrounding area—hasn't been lost. It’s a rare piece of 'Old Singapore' that still feels vibrant and relevant today.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The place is convenient for everyone one with every age to read or borrow the book for library member. There is give chance to exchange book as well which everyone could donate books been read and take others book donate by others. There is 3 floors building and providing books from children to adults with various topics.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
A 3-storey building comprising various collections at each level to suit every age group’s needs. A captivating local Singapore collection which hosts a multitude of authors and genres is something to check to out.

There is a “just returned” shelf the moment one enters so browsing through that for a quick borrow is effective. Displays based off of the theme for a particular length of time are seen at the staging area and book recommendations which follow the theme are also recommended. Very creative!

There is a lack of own ownership when it comes to the librarians checking on patrons to see if they’re following the rules. This has been observed quite a few times!

There was a bunch of noisy children in the Teen section nonetheless who were running around and creating a lot of commotion. There is a dedicated space for young readers at the 1st floor and some noise is to be expected there but the 2nd and 3rd floors are generally quiet. Not one librarian checked on them…

Also, there was a man lounging on the seat at a 180 degree angle, almost seeming like he was back home! A certain decorum is to be maintained when it comes to human behaviour but this individual had no social awareness and yet again, not one librarian checked up on him.

There was another NLB branch that’s currently undergoing revamping that was very strict with its policies. Librarians would chide patrons should they sit on the floor, even if their bags were kept on the seats. Should this not be the expected role of a librarian, among many others?

Last point to note is that if one is to use the browsing computer to search for a particular title, the shelf number may be inaccurate.

Edit: There are constant incidents of uninitiated noise and the worst bit is it’s the librarians themselves who are making it. Also, at this time, a lot of technicians move in and out yet completely forgetting library rules and etiquette. Do not visit if you’re looking for a quiet, peaceful read.
